published_prior_art.cite: GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 + standard Le Cam indistinguishable-law lower bound

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 is adjacent shared-completion LP bounds, not a null-vs-alternative collision frontier

published_prior_art.cite: GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 proves broad impossibility and a positive linear-in-means case, but not a sparse K-support TV collision modulus
GaoHarshawSavjeWang2026 Section 4 positive case is network-linear-in-means, not unknown sparse threshold-map LP separation

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 gives sharp graph-completion ADE bounds, not a testing-to-random-set-reporting rule

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 random-set sharp regions; SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 sharp exposure-misspecification bounds

novelty_flags.label: Related work / Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: BasseFellerToulis2019 is described as diagnosing exposure-map misspecification, but the verified Biometrika abstract presents a conditioning-mechanism framework for randomization tests under interference, not a misspecified-exposure-map frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship comparison cites HoshinoYanagi2023, BasseFellerToulis2019, and PuelzBasseFellerToulis2022 by bibkey only, without section/equation/page anchors for the exact missing TV-collision frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: AtheyEcklesImbens2018, Exact p-Values for Network Interference, is a direct randomization-inference comparator for network-interference testing and is missing from the survey.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Conjectures 1-2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposal alternates between full observable laws Q_{I,A,Y} with TV distance and finite conditional-mean linear images M(G,e)[0,1]^m; bounded conditional means alone do not define the full law or a finite TV LP unless outcomes are explicitly Bernoulli/deterministic or the object is changed to a mean-vector distance.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: C_K(P) is called the maximal convex invariant report, but the displayed exclusion property characterizes the minimal sharp convex report containing all colliding theta vectors; supersets would also be invariant.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the law/mean ambiguity by one convention: either assume Bernoulli outcomes with potential-outcome probabilities and define TV on the induced finite binary law, or define Lambda_K as distance between finite conditional-mean vectors.
Add location-level anchors for every flagship comparator, especially Hoshino-Yanagi, Basse-Feller-Toulis, Puelz-Basse-Feller-Toulis, and Athey-Eckles-Imbens.
Rewrite Conjecture 3 as a smallest closed convex invariant report / sharp convex hull statement and reserve 'maximal' for the class of admissible colliding structural explanations if needed.

Add a one-paragraph comparator table separating procedure papers, broad impossibility, network-misspecification robustness, and random-set partial-ID so Conjecture 1's frontier is not read as generic TV testing folklore.

recommended_next_step: Revise the observable-law convention first, then patch the comparator anchors and Conjecture 3 terminology before another novelty pass.
